---
layout: post
title: "America’s Scientific Dissension and Democratic Backsliding"
date: 2026-06-09
author: Ahnaf Choudhury
image: /assets/images/articles/cdc_atlanta.png
caption: CDC's Roybal campus in Atlanta, Georgia | Photo by James Gathany via Wikimedia Commons
categories: "national"
excerpt: "There has been a growing mistrust in scientific and non-partisan organizations perpetuated by the current administration."
---

When learning how to conduct research, most teachers tend to tell students that the internet is full of misinformation, but they can reliably trust certain websites including those which end in “.gov” since that indicates they are [officially a government website](https://get.gov/), which wouldn’t have blatant bias or misinformation . However, this has changed. The current administration has, in fact, promoted outright false information, [supporting ideas such as tylenol causing autism](https://www.fda.gov/news-events/press-announcements/fda-responds-evidence-possible-association-between-autism-and-acetaminophen-use-during-pregnancy), which has been refuted by scientific research numerous times. A key prong in this administration’s war against established consensus is the management of the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), led by Robert F. Kennedy Junior (RFK Jr). 

RFK Jr has been a controversial figure for a long time before he became part of the administration. For over a decade he has promoted unfounded scientific claims and was a vocal critic of Covid-19 restrictions. He also made wild claims about Covid, saying that it was [“ethnically targeted” and was made to attack Caucasians and Black people](https://www.nytimes.com/2023/07/15/us/politics/rfk-jr-remarks-covid.html) while sparing Ashkenazi Jews and Chinese, mirroring antisemetic and Sinophobic rhetoric.

He gained his current position as the secretary of HHS as a part of the Make America Healthy Again (MAHA) campaign, [which is modeled on Trump’s “Make America Great Again.”](https://www.politico.com/news/2026/04/06/poll-maha-beliefs-rfk-trump-00856922) However, RFK Jr, similar to much of the Trump administration, sees much of the science consensus as corporate capture. While it is true that corporations do have an influence on ideally independent government organizations through methods like lobbying, RFK Jr wrongly applies these concerns to things like vaccines, using these justifiable concerns as a red herring [to dismiss strong evidence](https://www.who.int/news-room/questions-and-answers/item/vaccines-and-immunization-vaccine-safety).

Research funded by groups that have a financial stake in the outcome should be taken critically, but various extensive studies by independent, not-for-profit organizations such as the [National Academy of Medicine](https://www.nationalacademies.org/news/statement-on-cdc-s-changes-to-guidance-on-vaccines-and-autism) or [EudraVigilance](https://www.ema.europa.eu/en/human-regulatory-overview/public-health-threats/vaccine-preventable-diseases-key-facts/vaccines-concerns-questions-false-claims) in Europe have proven that vaccines in general do not cause autism or other permanent side effects. Despite this, RFK Jr has long expressed doubt towards vaccines, and after becoming secretary of the HHS, he has projected his beliefs onto national guidelines and statements.  [He has fired all 17 members of the CDC’s Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices](https://www.npr.org/sections/shots-health-news/2025/06/09/nx-s1-5428533/rfk-jr-vaccine-advisory-committee-acip) (ACIP), the independent panel that recommends the nation's vaccine schedule, and replaced them with vaccine skeptics, although he was halted by the courts for likely violating procedure while doing so. 

He has issued [directives making optional various standard childhood vaccines, such as the flu, hepatitis A and B, rotavirus, and meningitis](https://apnews.com/article/childhood-vaccine-schedule-trump-rfk-hhs-9b8df9e2767c1261aaac4e2331e77fa3), which have protected the United States from seeing dangerous outbreaks of these diseases that kill thousands in other countries. Along with RFK Jr’s anti-vaccine advocacy through misinformation outside the government, like the Children’s Health Defense (CHD), [he has shifted official government stance on official websites away from agreed-upon definitive statements that vaccines are safe](https://www.pbs.org/newshour/health/cdc-vaccine-safety-webpage-changed-to-contradict-scientific-conclusion-that-vaccines-dont-cause-autism) to skeptical language that suggests that vaccines might not be safe. RFK Jr is using institutional trust to break down trust in genuinely beneficial programs. 

With the government contributing to the anti-vaccine campaign, anti-vax positions are much more likely to gain a foothold instead of being dismissed as conspiracy theories. Anti-vaxxers have already done incredible damage to public health and have allowed previously rare diseases to ravage communities. Measles is a disheartening example. This disease is not some childhood rash; [it is one of the most infectious diseases known to science](https://www.cdc.gov/measles/about/index.html), [hospitalizes one in every five people](https://www.cdc.gov/measles/signs-symptoms/index.html), [can cause pneumonia and occasionally swelling of the brain, destroys immunity to other diseases your body has previously acquired through vaccines or exposure](https://www.who.int/news-room/fact-sheets/detail/measles), [and has no definitive cure](https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/diseases/8584-measles). Such diseases can only be combatted with preventive measures like vaccines, and since it spreads extremely easily, any drop in vigilance can allow it to resurge. 

![][image1]  
[CDC chart on measles cases since the start of 2022 to the present](https://www.cdc.gov/measles/data-research/index.html).

Thankfully, the [Measles, Mumps, and Rubella (MMR) vaccine is 97% effective when administered twice as recommended](https://www.cdc.gov/measles/vaccines/index.html), and it had allowed measles to stop circulating in the United States by the year 2000\. However, thanks to a [fraudulent, evidence-impoverished paper by the British former doctor Andrew Wakefield,](https://www.bmj.com/content/340/bmj.c696) the MMR vaccine has become the cornerstone of the anti-vax movement. Thanks to misinformation campaigns, measles cases have risen from basically 0 to 2,000 cases in the first half of 2026\. [RFK Jr and his platform CHD were personally responsible for a deadly outbreak in Samoa](https://pulitzercenter.org/stories/samoas-tragedy-and-rfk-jrs-alleged-role). Two infants had died after they were administered the MMR vaccines because the nurses had mixed the vaccine with an expired muscle relaxant instead of water, (they were both prosecuted for manslaughter) but CHD pushed the false narrative that the vaccine itself was lethal, with RFK Jr personally coming to Samoa to push the narrative. Vaccination rates plummeted to 34%, and just a few months later, Samoa was hit with an outbreak that infected 5,700 people and killed 83, mostly young children who relied on herd immunity (others vaccinating and preventing the disease from spreading to those that cannot vaccinate). 

RFK Jr is part of Trump’s broader campaign to sow distrust in institutions, remove experts from their government positions, and replace established consensus with subjective opinions. Trump has frequently [undermined the credibility of established institutions](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7595043/) and has told followers to appeal to him instead of the institutions that democracy is built upon, such as the press. As a right-wing populist, Trump’s strategy heavily relies on undermining established institutions, but this populism weakens democracy itself. Trump is engaging in a form of [democratic backsliding, which eventually leads to the erosion of democratic institutions](https://www.brookings.edu/articles/understanding-democratic-decline-in-the-united-states/) through various methods, including targeting and politicizing independent personnel that would otherwise restrict the growth of authoritarianism. By undermining trust in experts, Trump has grown public support to replace them with loyalists, ensuring that independent and non-partisan institutions either have no real power or lose being non-partisan. By eliminating independent referees and making the populist persona and movement the source of truth, [democratic accountability is lost](https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/commentisfree/2018/jan/21/this-is-how-democracies-die#:~:text=The%20tragic%20paradox%20of%20the,even%20legally%20%E2%80%93%20to%20kill%20it) and we are at greater risk for totalitarianism. 

The change of official government platforms from outlets of verified science into parakeets for fringe science marks a dangerous form of public administration. By weaponizing institutional trust and turning the public against the agencies and processes designed to protect us, we have become part of the issue. It’s imperative that we push back and fight to defend evidence-based information and democratic accountability
